assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and 'Sub-task will be created to address this feedback.' The file subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description. report.md confirms in the Review Feedback Classification table: '30001 | reviewer-a | code change request | Yes -- transaction wrapping for soft_delete method'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and contains a '## Suggestive Language Analysis' section that explicitly analyzes three suggestive phrases: (1) '"should also add" -- The word "also" frames this as an additional recommendation beyond the core requirement', (2) '"would help" -- The conditional "would" indicates the reviewer is proposing a potential improvement, not identifying a defect', (3) '"Something like:" -- This phrase introduces the code example as one possible approach rather than the required solution'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task created. Nits are informational feedback that do not require tracked work.' No subtask-30003.md file exists. report.md confirms: '30003 | reviewer-a | nit | No'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not require code changes.' No subtask-30004.md file exists. report.md confirms: '30004 | reviewer-a | question | No'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: The only sub-task is subtask-30001.md. It contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (lines 28-29).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 31) with Comment ID, Author, File, and the full original comment text quoted: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' (lines 32-37).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are classification/reporting markdown files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) appear in the outputs directory. The report analyzes the existing PR diff but does not indicate any code modifications were made by the skill.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments are processed: individual classification files exist for comments 30001-30004 (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Feedback Classification table lists all 4 comments with their classifications. The report summary states '4 review comments classified'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The report concludes with findings and a standard footer. The overall result is 'WARN' with a recommendation for a sub-task, not a merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file); no existing tests modified or deleted |'. Also confirmed in the detailed section at lines 89-90: '**Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE** Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`).'.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '## Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section with three subsections: (1) '### CONVENTIONS.md Check' -- explains file contents are not available to verify conventions, (2) '### Codebase Pattern Check' -- explains only one migration visible, insufficient to establish pattern, (3) '### Upgrade Decision' -- explicitly states 'The suggestion is NOT upgraded' with 3 reasons. report.md also states: 'Comment 30002 (partial index suggestion) was evaluated for convention upgrade but remains a suggestion -- no verifiable convention or demonstrated codebase pattern supports upgrading it.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Final Classification: suggestion' and 'No sub-task created. Suggestions are informational feedback that do not require tracked work unless backed by a verifiable project convention or demonstrated codebase pattern.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30002 | reviewer-a | suggestion | No -- index recommendation; convention upgrade evaluated but not applied (no verifiable convention or codebase pattern)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR'. Detailed section at lines 85-87: '**Eval Quality -- N/A** No eval result reviews exist in the PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria detection heuristic (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' The Test Quality row shows 'PASS' composed of 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS. Test Documentation: PASS. Eval Quality: N/A' -- Eval Quality does not affect the combination.
